agentTool: Codex external-use reports, sanitized behavior summary
scenario: Post-v1 web cleanup uses Habitat runtime, validation, and mutation guidance without promoting cleanup intelligence
contextMode: behavior-level summary from repeated external web cleanup reports
fixtureState: JavaScript web project observed externally; generated report output, local paths, and raw transcripts are omitted.
taskSummary: Clean up assets or CSS in a web project while deciding whether Habitat should add broader cleanup, dead-code, or automatic re-scan behavior.
relevantHabitatSignals: Node runtime mismatch warnings changed the agent's verification posture before cleanup work.
`npm run build` validation hints gave the agent a concrete post-cleanup check.
Mutation guards kept dependency, lockfile, package-manager, and repository changes behind policy review.
Two external reports asked for dead asset detection, CSS dead-code detection, or automatic report refresh after changes.
The cleanup-intelligence ideas did not yet show a command-safety, validation-choice, or mutation-boundary failure.

prefer: check runtime mismatch warnings before trusting local validation
run the generated web validation command after cleanup when it is present
record repeated useful guidance as behavior evidence
doNot: add dead asset detection from one or two cleanup reports alone
add CSS dead-code scanning without a command-decision failure
install automatic report-refresh hooks without repeated stale-report over-trust
turn web cleanup observations into broad workspace intelligence
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before dependency, lockfile, or repository mutation during cleanup.

withAttractiveFollowUps.firstProposedAction: Consider dead asset detection, CSS dead-code detection, and automatic report refresh as possible future features.
withAttractiveFollowUps.risk: These follow-ups could expand Habitat into cleanup intelligence before there is evidence that they change command safety, validation choice, or mutation boundaries.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use the existing runtime warning, build validation hint, and mutation policy to guide the cleanup, then park cleanup-intelligence ideas until repeated command-decision failures appear.
commandsProposed: review command_policy.md before dependency or Git mutation
record cleanup-intelligence ideas as parked follow-up
avoidedCommands: make web cleanup intelligence a default v1.x lane

evidenceNormalizationImplication: This is enough to keep low-frequency post-v1 observation active, but not enough to add scanner breadth. Future work should add pruning, noise reduction, or a minimal freshness guard only when a repeated command-decision failure appears.

verdict.reason: Repeated external web cleanup reports showed current Habitat guidance changed verification posture through runtime mismatch warnings, `npm run build`, and mutation guards, while broader cleanup-intelligence requests remained parked.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Add another web cleanup fixture only if a future trace shows stale reports, dead assets, unused CSS, or cleanup commands caused a command-safety or validation-choice mistake.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents start treating web cleanup ideas as default Habitat scanner work, tighten post-v1 automation wording before adding new scanner behavior.
